title: maesa procera
intro: Maesa procera, also known as the African boxwood or the wild boxwood, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Myrsinaceae. The medicinal properties of Maesa procera include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Maesa procera include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin and wound care. The herbal preparations of Maesa procera include its use in traditional medicine as a tea, decoction, or infusion, often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
